verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Almagreira After work (16:30 start)
verdict.reason: Almagreira is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

conversationalSummary.text: For TODAY’s after-work window, Almagreira (aiming for a 16:30 start) is the best option, but it’s a bit of a lottery—Surfline consistency is lully. Wind looks mixed and a touch cross-off, and with an exposed beach break you’ll feel it quickly, especially on the mid-dropping tide. I’d check Surfline and MEO Beachcam before you leave and only commit if it looks clearly better than you’d expect; otherwise, don’t waste the drive.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Almagreira looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 43 kJ, 6 s period, lully 8/100, and wind is not fully clear.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully.
best.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 46 kJ, 8 s period, lully 12/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at mid tide can turn Belgas punchy and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a small window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 31 kJ, 8 s period, lully 7/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the reef shelves.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide helps a little, but it is not a perfect match.
warnings: 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the reef shelves

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 43 kJ, 8 s period, lully 14/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size
Cerro may feel too soft without more push
